name: Slice and Bake Oatmeal Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Cut four 14 x 12-inch pieces of waxed paper or plastic wrap.
3. Cream shortening, granulated sugar, and brown sugar in a large bowl until smooth.
4. Beat in eggs and vanilla until light and fluffy.
5. In a medium bowl, combine flour, salt, and baking soda.
6. Gradually stir the flour mixture into the egg mixture until blended.
7. Stir in oats, nuts, and raisins.
8. Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces.
9. Shape each piece into an 8 to 10-inch roll.
10. Wrap each roll in one piece of waxed paper or plastic wrap.
11. Place wrapped rolls in a plastic freezer container with a tight-fitting lid, or wrap airtight in a 14 x 12-inch piece of heavy-duty foil.
12. Label with date and contents.
13. Store in freezer until ready to bake.
14. To bake one roll, preheat the oven to 350°F and lightly grease two large baking sheets.
15. Cut the prepared frozen dough into 1/4 inch thick slices.
16. Place about 1 inch apart on the baking sheets and bake for 10 to 12 minutes until edges are browned and centers are slightly set.
17. Cool completely on wire racks.
18. Use within 6 months. Yield: 4 rolls of Slice & Bake Oatmeal Cookies or about 12 dozen.
